decisions.title_en: Pentagon signs classified AI deals with 7 firms (SpaceX, OpenAI, Google, NVIDIA, Reflection, Microsoft, AWS); Anthropic excluded
decisions.reason: story_key not in lookback. Event happened 2026-05-01, 1 day ago. Pentagon's first multi-vendor classified AI agreement at IL6/IL7 — sets precedent for AI in warfighter decision-making. Anthropic publicly snubbed.

decisions.title_en: NSA tests Anthropic Mythos to find Microsoft software flaws; Pentagon CTO Emil Michael says Anthropic still blacklisted but Mythos is a 'separate national security moment'
decisions.reason: Bloomberg broke 4/30 story that NSA quietly testing Mythos against Microsoft Corp. products; CNBC May 1 captured Pentagon CTO Emil Michael's statement. story_key distinct from prior anthropic-mythos-zerodays — that was about Mythos discovering CVEs in open source, this is about NSA government testing of Mythos against US enterprise tech. Event 2026-05-01.

decisions.title_en: Big 4 hyperscaler 2026 AI capex hits $725B (+77% YoY) after Apple/Microsoft/Amazon/Meta/Alphabet earnings synthesis
decisions.reason: Synthesis figure crystallized 4/30 after all Big 4 hyperscalers reported. Individual earnings (Microsoft, Amazon, Meta, Alphabet, Apple) were each covered separately on prior days, but the aggregate $725B figure with HBM-driven cost decomposition is a separate story_key. Within 5-day window.

decisions.title_en: Musk v. Altman trial: Musk testimony fully complete (Day 4 Thursday), Birchall answers off-limits question, Stuart Russell expert excused, Brockman confirmed for Monday May 4
decisions.reason: Returning story. Lookback shows trial Day 1-4 already published 2026-04-27 through 2026-04-30. New info for 5/2 briefing: Musk's testimony fully concluded Thursday afternoon, expert witness Stuart Russell was excused early without testifying (notable trial-strategy update), Jared Birchall answered an off-limits question while jury was out (potential mistrial implication), and Brockman confirmed as first witness Monday May 4. Substantively new courtroom developments distinct from prior daily recaps.

decisions.title_en: Florida AG Uthmeier expands criminal probe of OpenAI to include USF doctoral-student murders after suspect used ChatGPT to query disposing of remains
decisions.reason: story_key not in lookback. April 27 expansion of original FSU shooting probe to USF murders (Limon/Bristy case, Apr 16 missing). Uthmeier's office is first US state-level criminal investigation against an AI company over user actions. Within 5-day window (May 2 - April 27 = 5 days).

decisions.title_en: Apple Q2 FY26 earnings reaction May 1: stock +4% AH, Cook on Gemini-Siri partnership going well, Ternus calls AI 'incredible roadmap'
decisions.reason: Returning story. Already published 2026-04-30 with title covering Q2 results, $1112B revenue, $1000B buyback, Cook-to-Ternus transition, and HBM/Gemini-Siri timebombs. May 1 'reaction' coverage adds only stock movement (+4%) and Ternus quote, both already implied by prior day's coverage. No substantively new development.

decisions.title_en: Anthropic tops $30B run-rate, $1B+ enterprise customers double in 2 months
decisions.reason: Original Bloomberg/Anthropic announcement dated 2026-04-06. event_date is 26 days old (>5 day limit). The May 1 'reminder' coverage is editorial summary, not a new development.

decisions.title_en: Berkshire Hathaway 2026 annual meeting first without Buffett at podium; Abel takes over, AI as risk/opportunity panel topic
decisions.reason: Within 5-day window and novel, but primarily a Buffett/leadership transition story; AI is only a panel topic, not a meaningful AI development. Daily Tech & AI briefing scope filter — drop as not-AI-primary.

decisions.title_en: OpenAI ChatGPT Instant Checkout via Agentic Commerce Protocol (with Stripe) goes live
decisions.reason: OpenAI Instant Checkout / Agentic Commerce Protocol announcement is from late September 2025 (refreshed coverage in April). The latest movement is gradual rollout, no fresh news from 4/27-5/2 window. event_date older than 5 days.

decisions.title_en: SoftBank weighs $100B AI/robotics spinout 'Roze' for US IPO
decisions.reason: Reuters/FT first reported 4/29; CNBC followup 4/30. Although within 5-day window and not in lookback, the only concrete news is 'reportedly weighing $100B valuation' — speculative, no formal filing or commitment. Skipping as routine deal-rumor, would need formal filing or pricing to merit a card.

decisions.title_en: Google Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite: 2.5x faster response, 45% faster output gen
decisions.reason: Released 2026-04-22 at Google Cloud Next 2026. event_date 10 days old (>5 day limit).
